title: Create Simple Consulting Proposal with Labor Resource
objective: Create a Simple Consulting Proposal, copy the best practice template, update Labor Resources, calculate cost and price, and verify proposal totals.

steps.sourceAction: Navigate to app URL and log in
steps.automation: connectToTwentyFive reuses saved state or logs in with configured test credentials

steps.sourceAction: Enter title, dates, organization fields, and verify auto-populated values
steps.automation: Fill proposal setup fields using field-label scoped inputs and dropdowns

steps.sourceAction: Copy from best practice template and wait for proposal save
steps.automation: Verify template section, click Copy, wait for save success, and capture saved quote URL

steps.sourceAction: Open Project Scope and Labor Resources
steps.automation: Click tabs and verify WBS row / Labor Resources planning view

steps.sourceAction: Set Grade, Role, and FTE on the 1 Charge Code labor row
steps.automation: Use active-grid row and header/column scoped cell locators with ExtJS editor inputs

steps.sourceAction: Run Check & Save and verify final KPIs
steps.automation: Open Save menu, click Check & Save, wait for roll-up, and verify price/cost/margin text

runtimeVariances.runtimeBehavior: ProposalSetupPage appends a readable timestamp before entry so repeated automation runs create unique proposals.
The source proposal ID is recording-specific. The script waits for and reports the newly saved #quote URL instead of hardcoding this ID.
Auto-populates with the logged-in user. The recording used Praveen kumbar; this run uses the configured .env.local user (Tech User).
The source says Cost Center auto-populates after selecting RG23 - Spares. If the dev UI leaves it blank, the script explicitly selects REG CC-3 before continuing.

runtimeVariances.sourceValue: Template copy creates row 1 Charge Code
runtimeVariances.runtimeBehavior: The dev UI can open Labor Resources with no copied rows. The script clicks the grid add link and selects 1 Charge Code in the WBS cell before setting Grade, Role, and FTE.
